name: Chord Grid With Rhythmic Notation

releases.author: MathieuCGit
releases.description:
# Release Notes - Version 2.2.0

**Release Date:** December 18, 2025

This major release brings extensive new features for music notation, including fingerstyle patterns, pick strokes, pedagogical counting, measure numbering, transposition, ghost notes, and much more!

---

## 🎸 New Notation Features

### Fingerstyle Notation (`finger` directive)
Add fingerstyle patterns to your chord grids with automatic hand position indicators:
- Arrow indicators (↓↑) for playing direction
- Automatic pattern detection and generation

```chordgrid
finger:en
4/4 | C[4 88 4 88] |
```

or in french
```chordgrid
finger:fr
4/4 | C[4 88 4 88] |
```

### Pick-Stroke Notation (`pick` or `picks` or `picks-auto` directive)
Display alternating pick strokes for precision playing:
- Automatic alternating downstrokes (↓) and upstrokes (↑)
- Global subdivision detection (8th or 16th notes)
- Timeline-based algorithm ensuring musical accuracy
- Collision avoidance with ties and other elements

```chordgrid
pick
4/4 | C[4 88 4 88] |
```

### Ghost Notes Notation
Cross-shaped note heads (×) for muted/ghost notes:
- Syntax: suffix note value with `x` (e.g., `8x`, `16x`)
- Supports beams, ties, and tuplets
- Automatic flags on isolated ghost notes

```chordgrid
4/4 | Em[4 8x]G[8_4 4x] | D[4 8x]C[8_4 4x] 
```

### Pedagogical Counting (`count` directive)
Beat counting system for rhythm learning:
- Sequential numbering: 1, 2, 3, 4, &, etc.
- Three text sizes: tall (beats), medium (subdivisions), small (rests)
- Hybrid approach: adapts to regular and irregular meters
- User-defined beats respected (space-based grouping)

```chordgrid
count
7/8| C[88 88 888] |
```

### Measure Numbering (`measure-num` directive)
Optional measure numbering for easy navigation:
- `measure-num` - Start at measure 1, display at line starts
- `measure-num:5` - Start numbering at measure 5
- `measure-num:1-4` - Display every 4 measures

```chordgrid
measure-num:1-4
4/4 | C[4 4 4 4] | G[4 4 4 4] | Am[4 4 4 4] | F[4 4 4 4] |
```

---

## 🎵 Musical Enhancements

### Automatic Transposition (`transpose` directive)
Transpose all chords automatically:
- `transpose:+2` - Transpose up 2 semitones
- `transpose:-3` - Transpose down 3 semitones
- `transpose:+2,#` - Prefer sharps
- `transpose:-3,b` - Prefer flats
- Smart algorithm preserving chord quality and extensions

```chordgrid
transpose:+2
4/4 | C[4 4 4 4] | F[4 4 4 4] | G[4 4 4 4] |
```
Becomes: D, G, A

### Repeat Count Notation
Indicate number of repetitions with `:||xN` syntax:
- Display as small text to the right of repeat barline
- Support for single-digit (x3-x9) and double-digit (x10+) counts
- Works with full rhythm and `show%` modes

```chordgrid
4/4 ||: Am[4 4 4 4] | G[4 4 4 4] :||x3
```

### Enhanced Volta Brackets
Improved volta/endings notation:
- Multi-line volta support spanning across rendering lines
- `|.` syntax for explicit volta end markers
- Support for ranges (`|.1-3`) and lists (`|.1,2,3`)
- Closed vs open bracket rendering

```chordgrid
4/4 ||: C[4 4 4 4] |.1-2 F[4 4 4 4] :||.3 G[4 4 4 4] |
```

### Empty Measures
Create empty measures with `| |` syntax:
- Works with or without `measures-per-line` directive
- Special visual treatment (half width, no staff line)
- Compatible with voltas and barlines

```chordgrid
4/4 | C[4 4 4 4] | | | G[4 4 4 4] |
```

---

## 🎨 Visual & Layout Improvements

### Space-Based Grouping System (v3.0)
New default philosophy for beam grouping:
- **Space-based mode** (DEFAULT): User spaces control all beam breaks
- **Auto-beam mode**: Algorithmic breaking based on meter (binary/ternary)
- **Binary mode**: Force binary grouping (groups of 2 eighths)
- **Ternary mode**: Force ternary grouping (groups of 3 eighths)

```chordgrid
4/4 | C[888 88888] |
```

```chordgrid
auto-beam
4/4 | C[888 88888] |
```

```chordgrid
binary
6/8 | C[888 888] |
```

### Enhanced SVG Rendering
- Optimized margins: top=0px (compact), side=30px, bottom=25px
- Dynamic width calculation accounts for repeat counts and right-side elements
- Removed fixed height to prevent Obsidian margin issues
- Better responsive behavior

### Improved Beam Rendering
- Thicker eighth-note beams (4px instead of 3px)
- Increased beam spacing (7px instead of 6px)
- Longer beamlets (10px instead of 8px)
- Better visual clarity and readability

### Chord Positioning Refinements
- Aligned with note head left edge instead of stem center
- Dynamic collision avoidance with stems
- Proper sizing hierarchy based on context
- Stacked parentheses support for variations

---

## 🏗️ Architecture & Code Quality

### New Managers & Components
- **VoltaManager**: Multi-line volta coordination (accumulate-execute pattern)
- **PlaceAndSizeManager**: Intelligent element placement and collision detection
- **TimeSignatureRenderer**: Custom SVG digit paths for time signatures
- **Constants centralization**: 628 lines in `constants.ts`

### Code Improvements
- Line-scoped rendering architecture for multi-line grids
- Comprehensive English documentation and code comments
- Organized test suite: 450+ tests covering all features
- All documentation reorganized in `documentation/` folder

---

## 🐛 Bug Fixes

### Major Fixes
- **Repeat count SVG overflow**: Fixed `x8`, `x10` extending beyond SVG bounds
- **Note clipping**: Fixed with `measures-per-line` and repeat barlines
- **Barline rendering**: All barline types now render correctly with repeat measures
- **Volta collisions**: Fixed with repeat barlines and other elements
- **Empty measures**: Now render correctly without phantom measures
- **Tie rendering**: Multiple improvements for segment boundaries and special cases

### Visual Fixes
- Repeat barlines use classical notation style (thin 1.5px + thick 3px lines)
- Repeat dots properly centered on staff lines
- Excessive right margin reduced (60px → 20px)
- Double barline spacing restored to correct 6px

---

## 📚 Documentation

- Complete README overhaul with visual examples and step-by-step tutorial
- French README synchronized with English version
- ARCHITECTURE.md updated with latest code structure
- New documentation files:
  - GROUPING_CONVENTIONS.md - Rhythmic grouping reference
  - FINGERSTYLE_IMPLEMENTATION.md - Fingerstyle system details
  - Multiple time signatures test files and visual references
- Mermaid diagrams updated for GitHub compatibility

---

## 📊 Statistics

- **450+ tests** (was 284 in v2.1.0)
- **100+ commits** since v2.1.0
- **Multiple new test files** covering all features
- **Full backward compatibility** maintained

---

## 📥 Installation

For complete documentation and examples, see the [README](README.md).

releases.author: MathieuCGit
releases.description:
# v2.1.0 - Tuplets & Collision Management

## 🎉 Major Features

### CollisionManager System
- **Automatic collision detection** between chords, notes, stems, tuplets, rests, and time signatures
- **Smart vertical positioning** to avoid overlaps
- **Priority-based collision resolution** (fixed elements vs. mobile elements)

### Dynamic Time Signature Spacing
- Automatic calculation of time signature width based on content
- Adaptive left padding to prevent overlap with first measure
- Tighter, more elegant spacing (factor 0.53, margin 4px)

### Dotted Note Collision Avoidance
- Tie curves automatically raised to avoid dotted note dots
- Dots remain in standard position (right of note head)
- Collision detection between tie paths and dot bounding boxes

## 🧪 Comprehensive Testing

- **174 automated tests** with 100% pass rate
- **60+ tests** validating beams, ties, and tuplets across 12+ time signatures
- Coverage: 2/4, 3/4, 4/4, 5/4, 7/4, 5/8, 7/8, 6/8, 9/8, 11/8, 12/8, 15/16
- Tests for simple time, compound time, and asymmetric meters
- Edge cases: rests with beams/ties, cross-measure ties, tuplets with ties

## 🎨 Visual Improvements

- Time signature no longer overlaps first measure content
- Tuplet numbers automatically avoid chord symbols
- More compact overall layout without visual conflicts
- Tie curves intelligently avoid dotted notes

## 📐 Technical Details

- New `CollisionManager` class with full API for element registration and conflict resolution
- Extended `ElementType` union to include all renderable elements including `'dot'` for dotted notes
- Collision detection uses axis-aligned bounding boxes (AABB) with configurable margins
- `findFreePosition()` algorithm with spiral search pattern

## 📚 Installation

1. Download the 3 files attached to this release:
   - `main.js`
   - `manifest.json`
   - `styles.css`

2. Copy them to your Obsidian vault's plugin folder:
   ```
   <your-vault>/.obsidian/plugins/chord-grid/
   ```

3. Enable "Chord Grid" in Obsidian Settings → Community Plugins

## 🔗 Links

- [Full Changelog](https://github.com/MathieuCGit/ChordGrid_for_Obsidian/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md)
- [Documentation](https://github.com/MathieuCGit/ChordGrid_for_Obsidian/blob/main/README.md)
- [Architecture Guide](https://github.com/MathieuCGit/ChordGrid_for_Obsidian/blob/main/ARCHITECTURE.md)

releases.author: MathieuCGit
releases.description:
# 🎉 Version 2.0.1 - Architecture Refactoring

## ⚠️ Breaking Changes
Complete rewrite of the beam rendering system. The analyzer-based architecture is now the only rendering path.

## ✨ New Features
- **Cross-segment beaming**: Notes beam across chord boundaries when no space (e.g., `[8]G[8]`)
- **Multi-level beam support**: Proper rendering of 8th, 16th, 32nd, 64th notes
- **Smart beamlet direction**: Dotted notes influence partial beam direction
- **Clean architecture**: Parser → Analyzer → Renderer separation complete

## 🔧 Technical Changes
- Removed legacy `BeamAndTieAnalyzer.ts` from renderer (~300 lines)
- Removed `USE_ANALYZER_BEAMS` feature flag (analyzer always active)
- Simplified MeasureRenderer logic
- MusicAnalyzer now handles all beam calculations

## 📚 Documentation
- Updated ARCHITECTURE.md with final design
- Updated README with v2.0 features
- French documentation synchronized
- REFACTORING_PLAN.md marked as completed

## 🐛 Bug Fixes
- Beams no longer broken at chord segment boundaries
- Beamlet direction correct for dotted note patterns
- Isolated short notes display proper flags

## 📦 Installation

### For Obsidian Users
1. Download `main.js` and `manifest.json` from the Assets below
2. Copy them to `.obsidian/plugins/chord-grid/` in your vault
3. Reload Obsidian

### For Developers
```bash
git clone https://github.com/MathieuCGit/chord-grid.git
cd chord-grid
npm install
npm run build
releases.name: v2.0.1 - Complete Analyzer Architecture
